subject: mathematics

core_competencies.justification: The chapter's central organising activity is systematic counting and listing: students tally 45 raw responses into a subject frequency table, record gola preferences by colour into a structured display, and sort classmates into a two-way chess/cricket table by category membership. Each of these tasks requires the student to enumerate all cases within a defined constraint — precisely the systematic counting and listing described in this competency.

The Bal Mela pictograph tasks and the Colourful Golas questions require students to solve real daily-life problems using data — computing total food items sold across three days, identifying which day had peak sales, and deciding which gola to buy based on popularity. These are genuine daily-life problems involving counting operations on whole numbers, going beyond mere data organisation to purposeful use of data.

adjunct_competencies.justification: Reading the Bal Mela pictograph requires students to count icons across rows and add sub-totals for fruit chaats and sandwiches across three days — directly engaging addition of whole numbers to solve a daily-life comparison problem. Totalling across the frequency table also involves arithmetic aggregation on counted quantities.

Throughout the chapter, students compare whole-number frequencies to determine which subject is most or least liked, which gola colour is eaten most, and which sales day is highest — engaging the comparison of whole numbers in a data context, which is a core strand of this competency.

dissolution_test: This chapter builds the student's ability to organise and interpret collected data by systematically counting responses into frequency tables and pictographs and using the organised data to answer daily-life comparison questions.
